name: PR-HU 115 Trail to Ibón del Sen
name_original: Ibón del Sen desde San Juan de Plan — Parque Natural Posets-Maladeta
description: Jog the difficult PR-HU 115 Trail to Ibón del Sen, gaining 4442 feet over 10.3 miles to a stunning glacial lake.
region_intro: Jogging routes around San Juan De Plan are set within the Aragonese Pyrenees, offering diverse terrain for outdoor activities. The region is characterized by towering peaks, extensive pine and fir forests, and high alpine meadows. Glacial lakes, known as "ibones," are a prominent feature, often serving as focal points for running routes. The Cinqueta River also winds through the landscape, adding scenic variety. Best jogging routes around San Juan De Plan The most popular jogging route is Ibón de Plan Trail via GR 15, a difficult 2.1 miles (3.4 km) trail that takes about 32 minutes to complete. This route leads to the notable Ibón de Plan, a glacial lake. Another top favourite among local runners is PR-HU 115 Trail to Ibón del Sen, a difficult 10.3 miles (16.6 km) path. This trail offers demanding terrain within the Posets-Maladeta Natural Park, culminating at the Ibón del Sen. Local runners also love the Ibones de Barbarisa Trail from Collado de Sahún, a difficult 5.8 miles (9.3 km) trail leading through Pyrenean landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 44 minutes. Jogging around San Juan De Plan is defined by Pyrenean peaks, dense forests, and glacial lakes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from shorter, challenging paths to longer, more strenuous running routes. The routes in San Juan De Plan are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 15 reviews. More than 1,700 runners have used komoot to explore San Juan De Plan's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running trails are available around San Juan De Plan?
A: There are over 45 dedicated jogging routes around San Juan De Plan, offering a wide variety of terrain and challenges for runners. These routes are set within the stunning Aragonese Pyrenees.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for jogging routes in San Juan De Plan?
A: The routes in San Juan De Plan cater to various fitness levels. You'll find 3 easy routes, 12 moderate routes, and 32 difficult routes, ensuring options for both casual runners and experienced trail athletes. The region is known for its challenging mountain trails.

Q: What kind of landscape can I expect while running in San Juan De Plan?
A: Runners in San Juan De Plan can expect a diverse and dramatic landscape. The region is characterized by towering Pyrenean peaks, extensive pine and fir forests, high alpine meadows, and numerous glacial lakes known as 'ibones'. The Cinqueta River also winds through the area, adding scenic variety.

Q: Are there any notable natural landmarks or attractions along the running routes?
A: Yes, the area is rich in natural beauty. Many running routes feature stunning glacial lakes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/795895" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibón of Plan (Basa de la Mora)&lt;/a&gt;, which is known for its local legend. Other prominent features include the Cinqueta River and the characteristic reddish rock walls found in parts of the Pyrenean mid-mountain landscape.

Q: Can I find running routes that feature glacial lakes?
A: Absolutely. Glacial lakes, or 'ibones', are a prominent feature of the landscape and often serve as focal points for running routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e948177691/ibon-de-plan-trail-via-gr-15" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibón de Plan Trail via GR 15&lt;/a&gt; leads to the notable Ibón de Plan, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1395718900/pr-hu-115-trail-to-ibon-del-sen" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;PR-HU 115 Trail to Ibón del Sen&lt;/a&gt; culminates at the Ibón del Sen.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about the trails in San Juan De Plan?
A: The trails in San Juan De Plan are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 15 reviews. More than 1,700 runners have explored the area, often praising the stunning Pyrenean landscapes, the challenge of the mountain terrain, and the beauty of the glacial lakes.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly jogging routes in San Juan De Plan?
A: While many routes are challenging, there are 3 easy jogging routes available in San Juan De Plan. These paths offer a more gentle introduction to the region's beauty, suitable for families or those looking for a less strenuous run.

Q: When is the best time of year for trail running in San Juan De Plan?
A: The spring, summer, and autumn months generally offer the best conditions for trail running. In spring, the echinospartum horridum plant blooms, creating striking yellow hues. Summer provides warmer weather, while autumn brings beautiful foliage. Winter running can be challenging due to snow and ice in higher elevations.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the running trails in San Juan De Plan, especially within the natural park?
A: Many trails in the region are dog-friendly, but it's crucial to check specific regulations, especially within the Posets-Maladeta Natural Park. Generally, dogs should be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always carry water for your dog and clean up after them.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available?
A: Yes, the region offers several circular routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1388836306/pico-posets-loop-from-viados" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Posets Loop from Viadós&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging circular option for experienced runners seeking a long, strenuous run through the high mountains.

Q: What kind of terrain should I expect on the more difficult running routes?
A: The more difficult running routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e948284525/ibones-de-barbarisa-trail-from-collado-de-sahun" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibones de Barbarisa Trail from Collado de Sahún&lt;/a&gt;, often involve significant elevation gains, rocky paths, and varied surfaces typical of Pyrenean mountain trails. Sturdy trail running shoes and alpine experience are recommended for these routes.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the higher Pyrenean peaks?
A: Many routes in San Juan De Plan provide spectacular views of the Pyrenean peaks, some reaching between 2,000 and 3,000 meters. Trails within or bordering the Posets-Maladeta Natural Park, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1395709651/refugio-de-viados-to-ibon-de-millares-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refugio de Viadós to Ibón de Millares Trail&lt;/a&gt;, are particularly known for their panoramic mountain vistas.
tips:
Despite being relatively easy to access, this beautiful lake located at 2300 m altitude is not one of the most visited in the Pyrenees. However, it stands out for being located in a very clear glacial cirque, with an almost perfect overexcavation basin.

All Aragonese lakes are protected as they are included in the Inventory of Singular Wetlands, so any activity that could modify the quality of their waters is prohibited. While bathing is not specifically mentioned, it is understood that bathing with chemicals on the skin such as sunscreen, deodorants, perfumes or creams is negatively affecting the quality of the water, so it cannot be done.

You can read the protection decree and the list of wetlands included here: aragon.es/-/humedales-de-aragon

Although perhaps the most notable is its Romanesque parish church, expanded in the 16th century, the entire town is worth a walk through its steep streets flanked by houses with thick stone walls and slate roofs.

faqs.question: Where is the main trailhead for the PR-HU 115 Trail and is there parking?
faqs.answer: The primary starting point for the PR-HU 115 Trail is near the picturesque Puen Pecadó (or Puente de los Pecadores) bridge, which crosses the Zinqueta river. Parking is generally available in the vicinity, but it's advisable to arrive early, especially during peak season, as specific parking areas may fill up.

faqs.question: Is there an option to shorten the route to Ibón del Sen?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can significantly shorten the route. By driving further up a forest track to Salto l'Engrota, you can reduce the ascent to approximately 670 meters and decrease the overall distance, making the beautiful Ibón del Sen more accessible.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the way to Ibón del Sen?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and challenging. You'll start on paths and forest tracks at lower elevations, transitioning to high mountain meadows as you ascend. Near the ibón, expect a brief section of 'rock chaos'. The path involves a constant, sustained ascent with no flat sections, requiring good physical conditioning and strong ankles for the uneven ground. Mountain sports footwear is essential.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route to Ibón del Sen?
faqs.answer: You'll experience spectacular high-mountain scenery. The route passes through forests, high pastures with grazing sheep, and barrancos (ravines) with cascades. You'll also see traditional 'bordas' (shepherd's huts) and cultivated fields. The highlight is the stunning glacial lake, Ibón del Sen, nestled in a magnificent glacial cirque with turquoise, crystal-clear waters. There are also panoramic views of the surrounding valleys and the Cotiella massif.

faqs.question: Is the PR-HU 115 Trail to Ibón del Sen dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally permitted on trails in the Pyrenees, it's crucial to keep them on a leash, especially in high mountain pastures where livestock (like sheep) graze, and to protect local wildlife. Always pack out any waste. Given the challenging terrain and potential for snow, ensure your dog is fit for such conditions.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to jog this trail is typically from late spring to early autumn (June to October). Even in early summer, you might encounter snow in higher sections. Autumn offers beautiful colors, but be prepared for cooler temperatures. Winter conditions can make the trail impassable or require specialized equipment due to snow and ice.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route largely follows the Puén Pecadó-Ibones del Sen trail. It also briefly intersects with sections of the Sobrarbe. BTT. Ruta 4. Por el Valle de Chistau, Plan - Gistain - San Juan de Plan - Plan - Serveto, S-7 Sendero de la Ribera del Zinqueta, and Camín del Trestallo trails.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the PR-HU 115 Trail?
faqs.answer: The PR-HU 115 Trail is located within the Posets-Maladeta Natural Park. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking or jogging on marked trails within the park. However, always check the official park regulations before your visit, especially for any seasonal restrictions or specific rules regarding activities like camping or large groups.

faqs.question: Are there any resting points or refuges along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail passes by the Refugio Las Pardas, which can serve as a potential resting point during your journey to Ibón del Sen.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: If you are quiet and start early, especially in the higher sections, you have a chance to spot marmots and sarrios (chamois), which are common in the Pyrenean high mountains. Keep an eye out for various bird species as well.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for jogging this trail?
faqs.answer: Due to the varied and uneven terrain, specific mountain sports footwear with good ankle support is essential. Dress in layers, as weather conditions can change rapidly in the mountains. Bring plenty of water, high-energy snacks, sun protection (hat, sunglasses, sunscreen), a waterproof jacket, and a map or GPS device. Even in summer, a light jacket might be useful for higher altitudes.
